openQuestions.summary: Choose the exact implementation mechanism for loading parent shared resources from the audit smart router: parent-shared guard exception, hub-mediated load, or local shared-card proxy.

resolvedQuestions.summary: The current audit skill is mature and already includes the major 009 recommendations.
Net-new improvements are operational: router correctness, register loading, benchmark fixtures, evidence worksheet, backlog handoff and score calibration.
Router replay exposed a pseudocode loop defect and missing shared-register resource load.
Operator artifacts should be worksheets and handoff cards, not new workflow infrastructure.
Validation should add design-audit routing benchmark fixtures and keep manual playbook scenarios for behavior quality.
Scope-creep options were eliminated: duplicate basics, bulk corpus import, automated overlay claims, audit-as-fixer and new child skill.

keyFindings.title: Audit is mature; avoid broad expansion
keyFindings.summary: The live mode already has severity, evidence, scoring, a11y, hardening, AI tell, transform and report-template coverage.

keyFindings.title: Router pseudocode is not executable as written
keyFindings.summary: The parseable pseudocode loops over keyword strings as `(keyword, weight)` tuples. Fixing this is the smallest correctness improvement.

keyFindings.title: Shared register is required but absent from replayed resources
keyFindings.summary: Audit loading levels and workflow require the Brand-vs-Product register, but router replay for full-audit and transform prompts omitted it.

keyFindings.title: Evidence worksheet would reduce false confidence
keyFindings.summary: The model is clear, but a worksheet would force target, evidence, scan/probe and confirmed/inferred/not-assessed labels before scoring.

keyFindings.title: Backlog handoff keeps audit from becoming implementation
keyFindings.summary: A backlog-shaped asset can route accepted fixes to sk-code while preserving the audit boundary.

keyFindings.title: Audit benchmark artifacts are missing in this checkout
keyFindings.summary: Manual scenarios exist, but no 014-routing-benchmark/design-audit report was found. The operator-provided 82/100 score needs checked-in fixtures to become actionable evidence.

keyFindings.title: Anti-Patterns score ladder is the best small content addition
keyFindings.summary: The AI tell catalog is concrete, but a score ladder would make `/20` scoring more consistent across isolated vs systemic tells.

rankedRecommendations.title: Fix router keyword loop and shared-register loading
Add design-audit routing benchmark fixtures and report
Update manual playbook to require worksheet and replay evidence where available

ruledOutDirections.reason: Existing accessibility_performance and a11y_quick_fixes coverage is sufficient.
Existing critique_hardening and hardening_edge_cases split narrative and matrix well.
Evidence rules prohibit claims about tools that did not run.
Audit reports and routes; sk-code implements accepted fixes.
Prior 009 research rejected volume in favor of operational references and assets.
Current audit mode already owns the cross-cutting QA surface.
